content:
What happened # The Commitizen documentation (MkDocs + Material on GitHub Pages) shows fenced code blocks that fail to render correctly: ```bash appears as plain text inside a paragraph, and lines starting with # in code are even interpreted as Markdown headings (<h1>).
In the static HTML you can see the usual pattern: the fence opener ends up inside <p> instead of <div class="highlight"><pre><code>…, and # lines become ATX headings.
Where the bug is # In Pygments 2.20.0, HtmlFormatter runs html.escape(...) on filename. If the caller passes filename=None, html.escape(None) raises:
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'replace'
How the pieces connect # The docs are built with MkDocs + Material using fenced code blocks. Typical configs enable pymdownx.highlight and pymdownx.superfences. When a fence has no title, pymdownx.highlight builds HtmlFormatter and passes None to Pygments as filename=title. When highlighting fails, pymdownx.superfences cannot replace the fence with the expected HTML, and the leftover fence and body are parsed again as Markdown—hence “fence as plain text” plus “# becomes a heading.”
flowchart TD fence[fenced code, no fence title] ph[pymdownx.highlight] crash["Pygments 2.20.0: html.escape on None"] sf[pymdownx.superfences cannot replace fence] md[Markdown reparses body] bad["# lines become ATX headings"] fence --> ph ph --> crash crash --> sf sf --> md md --> bad If the same Markdown builds fine locally with mkdocs build but the gh-pages output looks wrong, look at build dependency versions (for example a lockfile resolving to pygments==2.20.0) or the build environment—not just the browser.
What to do # Pygments # The regression is tracked and fixed upstream; the fix is slated for 2.20.1.
Issue (closed): pygments/pygments#3076 Fix (merged): pygments/pygments#3078 (handle None before HTML escaping) Projects depending on Pygments (before fixed release) # If your project (for example, a docs build pipeline) depends on Pygments, apply a temporary mitigation until you can upgrade to a fixed release: pin Pygments below 2.20, for example:
pygments==2.19.2, or pygments<2.20 A similar mitigation appears in jj-vcs/jj#9233. Once the upstream fixed release is available and you upgrade, loosen the pin and re-run mkdocs build to confirm fenced code renders correctly.
Update # The Commitizen docs have now been fixed. See the merged PR: commitizen-tools/commitizen#1924.
Appendix: minimal Pygments repro # With pygments==2.20.0 installed, the snippet below should raise the AttributeError above; 2.19.2 usually does not:
from pygments.formatters.html import HtmlFormatter HtmlFormatter(filename=None) What I did # Tracing it in the build output # Noticed broken code blocks on the published site Local builds looked fine Compared environments and saw different Pygments versions Checked the gh-pages tree; HTML was already wrong, so the failure was in the build Compared HTML across Pygments versions Reproduced the failure manually and confirmed filename=None was the trigger Found the existing Pygments issue and merged fix Comment on the Commitizen issue # I left a comment on Broken docs #1922 with the summary below.
Root cause: pygments==2.20.0 regressed HtmlFormatter so that when filename is None, it calls html.escape() on None and crashes with AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'replace'. In our MkDocs stack, pymdownx.highlight ends up passing filename=None in the common case (no fence title), so the highlight step fails; pymdownx.superfences then doesn’t replace the fence, and the remaining text is parsed as normal Markdown — which is why fenced bash blocks and lines starting with # show up as plain text / headings. Upstream: - Issue: https://github.com/pygments/pygments/issues/3076 - Fix (merged): https://github.com/pygments/pygments/pull/3078 (targeted for Pygments 2.20.1) Possible mitigation until a Pygments release with the fix is available: pin pygments<2.20 for the docs build (similar approach: https://github.com/jj-vcs/jj/pull/9233).
summary: What happened # The Commitizen documentation (MkDocs + Material on GitHub Pages) shows fenced code blocks that fail to render correctly: ```bash appears as plain text inside a paragraph, and lines starting with # in code are even interpreted as Markdown headings (<h1>).
title: Commitizen broken docs: fenced code and Pygments 2.20.0

content:
This page collects my open-source contributions and the projects I care about most.
CPython # python/cpython The Python programming language Python 72183 34383 CPython is the reference implementation of Python, carried forward by a large, active community of contributors. I started contributing after PyCon Taiwan and the sprint events run by Eddie Kao (高見龍). See also the introduction chapter of 為你自己學 Python.
Open PR # Work in progress:
gh-139819: rlcompleter – avoid suggesting attributes not accessible on instances Tighten rlcompleter so tab completion skips attributes that are not accessible on instances Status: still waiting for review—quiet thread Merged PR # Merged into CPython:
gh-139487: doc(enum): add missing imports for standalone doctest examples Add missing imports to standalone doctest examples in the enum docs gh-139743: avoid import-time print in test_sqlite3 that leaks into help(‘modules’) Fix sqlite3 tests so import-time output does not pollute help('modules') Commitizen # commitizen-tools/commitizen Create committing rules for projects 🚀 auto bump versions ⬆️ and auto changelog generation 📂 Python 3368 334 Commitizen is a Python toolkit for writing consistent Git commit messages, with room for automation, plugins, and packaging workflows. Wei Lee—who got me into the project—is Taiwan’s only Apache Airflow PMC member. His post on the path: Becoming an Airflow PMC Member.
Open PR # Work in progress:
Feat: Validate message_length_limit is non-negative Require message_length_limit >= 0 explicitly Refactor: Doc(images) VHS tapes with shared snippets Refactor VHS tape scripts used for documentation images Extract shared snippets to remove duplication and make the docs easier to follow Feat: add live subject preview for interactive commit (–preview) Add a live preview for the commit subject in interactive mode Fix and improve test: message_length_limit Fix and extend tests around the message_length_limit setting Merged PR # Merged into Commitizen:
docs: document and demo use_shortcuts keyboard shortcuts Improve use_shortcuts documentation and examples CI: make release workflows fork-friendly Adjust GitHub Actions triggers so forks do not run release workflows unintentionally Doc: Clarify cz_customize deprecation warning with rationale link Clarify official docs around the cz_customize deprecation Resolve tempfile path spaces issue in git commit function My first Commitizen contribution: fix temp-file paths that contain spaces in the Git commit flow PyCon Taiwan # PyCon Taiwan promotes Python and open source in Taiwan with a major conference each year. I joined as a volunteer in 2025.
PyCon TW 2025: Program team, Communications team PyCon TW 2026: Program team, Communications team Articles # Think of this section as an open-source diary—short notes on contributions large and small.
